cards.back: A decision tree is a tree-like data structure that is used to make decisions based on the input values. It is a simple and intuitive way to solve problems, but it may not always be the most efficient.
P refers to the set of decision problems that can be solved in polynomial time, i.e., their solution can be computed in time proportional to the size of the input.

NP stands for 'nondeterministic polynomial time', referring to a set of decision problems that can be solved in polynomial time by a nondeterministic Turing machine, which can try all possible solutions in parallel.
An NP-complete problem is a decision problem that is at least as hard as the hardest problems in NP, meaning that it can be reduced to any other NP-complete problem in polynomial time.

A reduction is a transformation from one problem to another such that if the transformed problem can be solved, then the original problem can also be solved. In the context of NP-completeness, reduction is used to show that a problem is at least as hard as another NP-complete problem.
